claim: Skills are evolving from prompt fragments into explicit reusable infrastructure.
title: Portable skills are becoming reusable agent infrastructure
why_it_matters: Supports promoted capabilities that persist beyond one run.

claim: Portable skill metadata and delegation boundaries are an active design problem.
title: Delegation boundaries need explicit metadata
why_it_matters: Supports adding a promotion queue instead of ad hoc capability notes.

claim: Durable session and tracing semantics are now baseline expectations.
title: Sessions and tracing are table stakes in agent runtimes
why_it_matters: EvolveX already traces live runs, but it still needed a durable cross-run growth ledger.

claim: Always-visible status, queue state, and review surfaces matter for long-running agent workflows.
title: Persistent HUDs improve operator trust
why_it_matters: Supports a VS Code-like operator console instead of a passive dashboard.
